fix: treat Geant4 kInfinity as no-intersection in TraceRay (#255)

Geant4's kInfinity (CLHEP) is 9.9999e+99, a large but finite double.
std::isinf() returns false for it, so rays where DistanceToIn returns
kInfinity (no intersection) were being treated as valid hits.

This caused SurfaceNormal to be evaluated at kInfinity * direction,
a nonsensical point far outside the solid, producing garbage normals
and false surface-normal mismatches.  For G4TwistedTubs (dphi=120°),
this accounted for all 1381 reported surface-normal failures.

Fix:
- DistanceString: also print "kInfinity" for values >= kInfinity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct G4ScaledSolid exit normals in DistanceToOut fixture (#253)

* fix: correct G4ScaledSolid exit normals in DistanceToOut fixture

G4ScaledSolid::DistanceToOut (Geant4 bug, present through at least 11.4.1)
calls fScale->TransformNormal() on the exit normal returned by the unscaled
base solid.  TransformNormal maps normals from global→local frame, but the
base-solid normal (solNorm) is already in the local (unscaled) frame.  The
correct transform is InverseTransformNormal() (local→global), which applies
the inverse-transpose of the scale matrix (divide each component by its
scale factor).  G4ScaledSolid::SurfaceNormal() uses InverseTransformNormal()
correctly, explaining why SurfaceNormal yields 0 mismatches while
DistanceToOut exit normals show 2039/2048 failures for the non-uniformly
scaled sphere fixture (semi-axes 15×20×5 mm).

Fix in G4OCCT: introduce G4ScaledSolidFixed, a minimal subclass that
overrides DistanceToOut to recompute the exit normal via SurfaceNormal()
at the exit point.  SurfaceNormal() is correctly implemented in Geant4,
so this gives the geometrically correct outward normal without any
modification to the Geant4 source tree.  The fixture builder now constructs
G4ScaledSolidFixed instead of G4ScaledSolid for native-solid comparisons.

Remove the broad G4ScaledSolid xfail in fixture_validation.cc (reason was
stale: 'not implemented yet').  Add a comment explaining the Geant4 bug and
the workaround.  Document the bug and fix strategy in provenance.yaml.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

* Update src/tests/fixtures/geometry/wrapper-decorator/G4ScaledSolid/scaled-sphere-nonuniform-v1/provenance.yaml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: test-bridged 2-hop graph expansion for cross-file chains

When a test file ranks in the top-10 and connects via graph to a
non-test source file (hop1), that source file is now expanded one
further hop. Recovers cross-file chains like:
  prompt-builder.test.ts → prompt-builder.ts → orchestrator.ts

Targeted design: only fires on test→source paths, avoiding the
scope leaks that occur with unrestricted depth=2 expansion.

Also: extracted isTestFile() as a private class method so both
processRawResults and expandWithGraphNeighbors share the same logic.

Tests: 712/712 passing (+1 new test for test-bridged 2-hop case)

* fix(relay): address reviewer feedback on backoff and multi-relay

Seven issues from copilot-pull-request-reviewer:

1. isinstance PEP-604 union: change OSError | ... to tuple form so it
   works correctly on all Python versions.

2. RelayClient empty list: validate relay_urls is non-empty and contains
   only non-empty strings; raise ValueError with clear message.

3. publish() docstring: fix "returns as soon as at least one accepts" —
   actual behavior fans out to all relays; update doc to match.

4. Profile.load() coercion: handle default_relays as bare string (wrap in
   list), strip non-string entries, fall back to _DEFAULT_RELAYS when
   the result is empty.

5. poll_for_pair_response() reconnect backoff: track per-relay failure
   counts; apply _backoff_delay() before reconnecting to a relay that had
   a transient error, avoiding thundering-herd reconnects on rate-limited
   relays.

6. _is_rate_limited duplication: import and use relay._is_rate_limited()
   in publish_pair_request() instead of the inline reimplementation.

7. last_checked per relay: receive command now tracks last_checked for
   every configured relay URL and uses the oldest timestamp as the since
   filter, preventing missed events from secondary relays.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

single-entry list even for fresh profiles. Change the option default to

new profiles now inherit _DEFAULT_RELAYS = [damus, nos.lol] from the
dataclass field default.

nothing was printed. Assigns ship/user variables that were computed but
discarded, and renders each section with rich (greeting, systems check,

* fix(relay): address reviewer feedback on backoff and multi-relay

Seven issues from copilot-pull-request-reviewer:

1. isinstance PEP-604 union: change OSError | ... to tuple form so it
   works correctly on all Python versions.

2. RelayClient empty list: validate relay_urls is non-empty and contains
   only non-empty strings; raise ValueError with clear message.

3. publish() docstring: fix "returns as soon as at least one accepts" —
   actual behavior fans out to all relays; update doc to match.

4. Profile.load() coercion: handle default_relays as bare string (wrap in
   list), strip non-string entries, fall back to _DEFAULT_RELAYS when
   the result is empty.

5. poll_for_pair_response() reconnect backoff: track per-relay failure
   counts; apply _backoff_delay() before reconnecting to a relay that had
   a transient error, avoiding thundering-herd reconnects on rate-limited
   relays.

6. _is_rate_limited duplication: import and use relay._is_rate_limited()
   in publish_pair_request() instead of the inline reimplementation.

7. last_checked per relay: receive command now tracks last_checked for
   every configured relay URL and uses the oldest timestamp as the since
   filter, preventing missed events from secondary relays.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(init): default to two-relay list when --relay not explicitly provided

Previously `aya init` always called `p.default_relay = relay` with a
hardcoded wss://relay.damus.io default, collapsing default_relays to a
single-entry list even for fresh profiles. Change the option default to
None and only set default_relay when the user explicitly passes --relay;
new profiles now inherit _DEFAULT_RELAYS = [damus, nos.lol] from the
dataclass field default.

commit.message:
feat(server): add block interaction validation for build height, spawn protection, and replacement

Add three vanilla-compliant validations to block breaking and placement:

1. Build height validation: reject block actions outside overworld limits
   (y < -64 or y > 319) with acknowledgement but no state change.

2. Spawn protection enforcement: non-operator players cannot break or place
   blocks within spawn_protection radius (Chebyshev distance) of the world
   spawn. Configurable via gameplay.spawn_protection (default: 16, 0 disables).

3. Block replacement validation: placement only succeeds if the target
   position contains air (state ID 0). Non-air positions trigger a resync
   to correct client prediction.

Add spawn_protection field to ServerContext, populated from
GameplayConfig at startup. Include 13 new unit tests covering all three
validation paths.
